channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Deepfake porn is a labor issue
item.description: Last month, First Lady Melania Trump used her first public remarks of President Trump’s second term to voice her support for the Take It Down Act, a bipartisan bill aimed at curbing deepfake “revenge porn.” She was joined by Elliston Berry, a Texas teenager who was a victim of deepfake porn.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Wohl, Fast Company

item.title: New hands-on lab makes it easy to explore cloud configurations and workload migrations
item.description: For those that attended VMware Explore in Las Vegas and Barcelona, there was a new self-paced hands-on lab released exclusively for the attendees to experience Google Cloud VMware Engine while at the events.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft said it is “slowing or pausing” some of its data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio, the latest sign that the demand for artificial intelligence technology that drove a massive infrastructure expansion might not need quite as many powerful computers as expected.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meta helped build China’s DeepSeek: Whistleblower testimony
item.description: Former Meta executive Sarah Wynn-Williams is set to testify before the US Senate Judiciary Committee on Wednesday, revealing how the company’s AI model, Llama, played a critical role in accelerating China’s AI capabilities — particularly contributing to the rise of DeepSeek.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google Unveils Ironwood Chip to Accelerate Generative AI Workloads
item.description: Google's new chip, Ironwood, is designed to accelerate generative AI workloads, especially inferencing. The inferencing-only semiconductor market is projected to be $191 billion in size by 2030.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google Enhances AlloyDB with AI Capabilities
item.description: Google updates its fully managed database-as-a-service (DBaaS) AlloyDB with new AI capabilities, aiming to help developers build applications underpinned by generative AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google adds open source framework for building agents to Vertex AI
item.description: Google is adding a new open source framework for building agents to its AI and machine learning platform Vertex AI, along with other updates to help deploy and maintain these agents.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: TikTok extension, potential deal may violate law: Top Senate Democrat
item.description: The top Democrat on the Senate Intelligence Committee warned Monday that President Trump’s additional extension for TikTok’s parent company to divest from the app, as well as a potential divestment deal on the table, may violate the law. In a letter to Trump, Sen. Mark Warner (D-Va.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Musk calls Navarro 'moron' after 'demonstrably false' claims about Tesla
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk slammed President Trump's trade adviser Peter Navarro on Tuesday, calling him "truly a moron" in their latest spat over the administration's new import taxes. "Navarro is truly a moron.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Beware these 10 malicious VS Code extensions
item.description: Developers using Microsoft’s Visual Studio Code (VSCode) editor are being warned to delete, or at least stay away from, 10 newly published extensions which will trigger the installation of a cryptominer.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Microsoft workers say they've been fired after 50th anniversary protest over Israel contract
item.description: Microsoft has fired two employees who interrupted the company's 50th anniversary celebration to protest its work supplying artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military, according to a group representing the workers.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: World’s first AI-powered industrial super-humanoid robot
item.description: Mech the super-humanoid robot can lift up to 132 pounds and is designed to tackle stressful and repetitive tasks that often lead to workplace injuries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to free up space on your Android
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy offers some of his easy expert tips to free up storage space on your Android if your device is running short on storage.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI vs. pro gambler's $1 million March Madness bet coming down to Duke and Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — The $1 million March Madness wager between a pro gambler and an artificial-intelligence site will be decided by the winner of the Duke-Houston game in the Final Four. The Vegas bookies project the gambler, who picked 5 1/2-point favorite Duke, should win.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates reflects upon a 50-year-old computer code that reshaped technology
item.description: Even as he grows older, Microsoft founder Bill Gates still fondly remembers the catalytic computer code he wrote 50 years ago that opened up a new frontier in technology.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Nintendo Switch 2
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Nintendo's Switch 2 is bigger and better than its predecessor of eight years past as the video game developer pushes to bring its brand into “a new era" where social experiences are at the forefront.
item.dc:creator: RYAN KRYSKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
